Acceptance criteria: - [x] IsolationConfig frozen dataclass with sensible defaults (SandboxType.NONE, TokenTier.STANDARD) - [x] AgentSandbox protocol defined with @runtime_checkable - [x] NullSandbox satisfies protocol, returns command unchanged - [x] YAML graphs with isolation: parse at graph, workstream, task, and agent levels - [x] Four-level inheritance: agent overrides task overrides workstream overrides graph; unset fields inherit - [x] Graphs without isolation: use defaults (zero behavior change) - [x] pixi run check passes (1032 tests, 43 new) - [x] All existing tests pass unchanged - [x] E2E regression: diamond_4_workstreams_auto_merge.yaml passes

Scope: - CredentialProvider protocol: resolve(tier: TokenTier) -> dict[str, str] - FileCredentialProvider: reads tiered credentials from a YAML file, merges defaults with tier-specific env vars - NullCredentialProvider: returns empty dict (default for SandboxType.NONE) - Credential file schema:

  standard:

  elevated:
    GH_TOKEN: ghp_zzzz

- Wire into sandbox flow: SandboxContext.env_vars populated from credential resolution via TmuxTaskLauncher - Wire NullCredentialProvider into build_standard_runner() as default

• Branch: feat/oci-sandbox
  • Note: Renamed SandboxType.CONTAINER → SandboxType.OCI for naming consistency with OciSandbox. Added ContainerRuntime enum (DOCKER, PODMAN) to replace the raw string runtime field on IsolationConfig.

Scope: - Rename SandboxType.CONTAINER → SandboxType.OCI across codebase - ContainerRuntime enum in config.py, IsolationConfig.runtime changed from Optional[str] to Optional[ContainerRuntime] - _parse_optional_container_runtime() in builder (same pattern as _parse_optional_sandbox_type) - ops/docker.py: thin subprocess wrappers — is_available(), image_exists(), network_exists(), network_create(), network_remove(), stop(), rm(), build_run_command() - ops/git.py: worktree_git_dir() — reads worktree .git file to find main repo .git/ path - OciSandbox implementation: - wrap_command(): builds docker run -it --rm with bind mounts (worktree, signal dir, .git dir read-only), env vars, network - setup(): validates Docker available, creates network if needed - teardown(): stops + removes container, swallows errors - Per-task sandbox selection in builders.py: _make_launcher() factory inspects IsolationConfig.sandbox_type to choose NullSandbox or OciSandbox

Acceptance criteria: - [x] ops/docker.py wrappers follow ops/tmux.py pattern - [x] OciSandbox.wrap_command() produces correct docker run string with all bind mounts and env vars - [x] Git dir resolution reads .git file and extracts gitdir path - [x] Worktree, signal dir, and .git dir mounted at same absolute paths - [x] OciSandbox.setup() fails fast if Docker not available - [x] Container name includes task_id for debuggability - [x] pixi run check passes (Docker ops tests use subprocess mocking)

docs.text:
• Branch: TBD
  • Depends on: PR F1
  • Details: docs/sprints/supplementary-info-agent-isolation.md

Scope: Seven issues discovered during F1 e2e testing that make the reset/run/debug cycle slow and painful. All must be resolved before continuing with F2 e2e testing.

docs.title: Risks and mitigations
docs.text: Risk Impact Mitigation Git worktree .git file contains absolute host path Bind mounts won't work if paths differ Mount at same absolute path inside container Docker TTY passthrough may confuse wait_for_tui_ready() Agent launch hangs Test TTY flow manually; use --quiet Docker flags Credentials visible via docker inspect and agent logs Token leakage Document risk; future PR to scrub token patterns Docker not installed on user's system OciSandbox fails NullSandbox remains default; is_available() check with clear error Container startup latency Slower task launch Pre-pull image; tasks are minutes long so seconds of overhead is acceptable

docs.title: Supplementary Info — Agent Isolation Sprint (PR Fcleanup)
docs.text: Detailed descriptions of the seven issues discovered during PR F1 e2e testing. Each section describes the problem, how it manifested, and the impact on the e2e workflow.

docs.title: 1. reset_graph leaves stale local branches and worktree refs
docs.text:
Problem: reset_graph.py removes the worktree directory (shutil.rmtree) and the workflow directory, but does not: - Run git worktree prune to clean up git's worktree registry - Delete local branches created during the run

How it manifested: After every pixi run e2e-reset, the next pixi run e2e failed with:

_WorkspaceIntegrationError: Failed to create branch for task 'hello_fn':
  Command '['git', 'branch', '-f', '...', '...']' returned non-zero exit status 128.

Because git still considered the branch "used by" the now-deleted worktree. Required manual git worktree prune && git branch -D ... after every reset.

Impact: This was the single biggest time sink — every failed e2e attempt required ~4 manual commands to fully clean up.

Fix direction: Add git worktree prune and local branch deletion (all branches matching agentrelay/<graph>/*) to reset_graph.py's execute_reset() function, after the directory removal step.

Relevant code: src/agentrelay/reset_graph.py — execute_reset() function, after shutil.rmtree(plan.worktree_dir).

docs.title: 2. Container UID mismatch (agent=1001 vs host=1000)
docs.text:
Problem: The Docker image creates user agent with UID 1001. The host user (duane) is UID 1000. Ubuntu 24.04 base images include a pre-existing ubuntu user at UID 1000.

Bind-mounted directories (worktree, signal dir) are created by the host user (UID 1000) with mode 0775. The container's agent user (UID 1001) is not in the host user's group, so it cannot write to these directories.

How it manifested: Agent got EACCES: permission denied when trying to write files, commit, or write signal files (.done, .failed, ops_concerns.log).

Current workaround: --group-add with host GID gives the container process supplementary group access to host-owned directories. This works for the forward direction (container writing to host dirs) but not the reverse (host cleaning up container-created files — see item 5).

Impact: Required multiple iterations to diagnose. The --user approach (running as host UID) fixed permissions but broke the Claude Code TUI rendering and the HOME directory resolution.

Fix direction: In the Dockerfile, remove the ubuntu user (userdel ubuntu) and create agent with UID 1000. This aligns UIDs so no --group-add or --user workarounds are needed, and container-created files are owned by the host UID.

Relevant code: - docker/base/Dockerfile — user creation - src/agentrelay/sandbox/implementations/oci_sandbox.py — --group-add in wrap_command()

docs.title: 3. Claude Code first-run prompts consume kickoff in containers
docs.text:
Problem: Every container launch triggers Claude Code's interactive first-time setup prompts: - API key confirmation - Folder trust confirmation - Possibly other first-run dialogs

Because containers are ephemeral (--rm), every run is a "first time." The orchestrator sends the kickoff prompt ("Read instructions.md and follow the steps exactly.") via tmux send-keys, but this text is consumed by the startup prompts before Claude Code's main input is ready.

How it manifested: Claude Code launched and completed its startup prompts, then sat waiting for input. The orchestrator showed "waiting for completion signal" indefinitely. Required manual entry of the kickoff prompt.

Impact: Every e2e run required manual interaction — negates the automation value of the orchestrator for containerized agents.

Fix direction (in priority order): 1. Suppress interactive prompts: Investigate Claude Code CLI flags or env vars that skip first-run setup. Pre-seed config files in the image (e.g., write ~/.claude/settings.json with trust and API key confirmation during docker build). 2. Pass prompt as CLI argument: Use claude -p "Read ..." or claude --prompt "..." instead of tmux send-keys, bypassing the timing issue. 3. Container warm-up step: Run Claude Code briefly in OciSandbox.setup() to complete first-run initialization before the real task launches.

Relevant code: - src/agentrelay/agent/implementations/tmux_agent.py — send_kickoff() (line 85) - src/agentrelay/sandbox/implementations/claude_code_adapter.py — command construction - docker/framework/claude-code/Dockerfile — could pre-seed config

docs.title: 4. Reset doesn't clean up Docker containers/networks from failed runs
docs.text:
Problem: When an e2e run fails mid-execution (e.g., the agent crashes, the orchestrator is killed with Ctrl+C, or a container error occurs), Docker containers may still be running and the Docker network may still exist. The next pixi run e2e fails because: - The container name is already in use - The network already exists (though run_graph.py handles this better now)

reset_graph.py has Docker cleanup logic (added in PR E / #144) that stops/removes containers by label and removes the network. However, this cleanup is best-effort and can miss containers that exited but weren't removed (no --rm didn't apply, or the container was killed).

How it manifested: After Ctrl+C'ing a stuck run, the next attempt sometimes failed with network or container name conflicts.

Impact: Required manual docker stop / docker rm / docker network rm between attempts.

Fix direction: Make the reset Docker cleanup more robust: - Always run docker ps -a --filter label=agentrelay.graph=<name> and force-remove all matching containers - Always attempt network removal - Add Docker cleanup to the e2e-reset script as well (currently only in reset_graph.py)

Relevant code: - src/agentrelay/reset_graph.py — Docker cleanup section - tools/e2e_reset.sh — could add Docker cleanup

docs.title: 5. Reset fails on container-created files (PermissionError)
docs.text:
Problem: Files created inside the container by the agent user (UID 1001) are owned by UID 1001 on the host filesystem. When reset_graph.py tries to shutil.rmtree() the worktree directory, it fails with PermissionError on these files because the host user (UID 1000) doesn't have write permission to UID 1001-owned files.

How it manifested: 

PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied:
  '.worktrees/isolation-basic-oci/default/.pixi'

Required sudo rm -rf to clean up.

Impact: Every reset after a partially successful run required sudo.

Fix direction: This is largely resolved by fixing item 2 (UID alignment). If the container runs as UID 1000, all files it creates are owned by UID 1000, and the host user can delete them normally.

As a defense-in-depth measure, reset_graph.py could also catch PermissionError during rmtree and log a warning with instructions (or attempt cleanup via docker run --rm -v ... rm -rf ... using the container runtime itself).

Relevant code: src/agentrelay/reset_graph.py — execute_reset(), shutil.rmtree() calls.

docs.title: 6. E2e script coupled to target repo's pixi env
docs.text:
Problem: tools/e2e_run.sh (line 71) does: 

cd "$TARGET_REPO"
exec pixi run python -m agentrelay.run_graph "$GRAPH_ABS" "$@"

This runs the orchestrator via the target repo's pixi environment, which requires agentrelay to be installed as a dependency in the target repo's pixi.toml. If the target repo removes or changes that dependency, the orchestrator breaks.

How it manifested: After temporarily removing the agentrelay path dependency from agentrelaydemos' pixi.toml (to fix an in-container pixi error), the e2e script itself broke: 

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'agentrelay'

Required reverting the target repo change to restore orchestrator functionality.

Impact: The target repo's dependency management is tightly coupled to the orchestrator's ability to run. Changes to the target repo can break the orchestrator.

Fix direction: Change e2e_run.sh to run the orchestrator from the agentrelay repo's pixi environment instead: 

cd "$TARGET_REPO"
exec pixi run --manifest-path "$REPO_ROOT/pixi.toml" \
  python -m agentrelay.run_graph "$GRAPH_ABS" "$@"

Or set PIXI_PROJECT_MANIFEST to point to the agentrelay repo. The target repo's pixi env is only needed by agents (inside containers or worktrees), not by the orchestrator.

Similarly update e2e_reset.sh and e2e_check.sh.

Relevant code: - tools/e2e_run.sh — line 71 - tools/e2e_reset.sh — similar pattern - tools/e2e_check.sh — similar pattern

docs.title: 7. Git credential helper not configured in container
docs.text:
Problem: The container has GH_TOKEN injected as an env var, and gh auth status shows authentication is working (gh CLI reads GH_TOKEN). However, git push/pull over HTTPS doesn't use GH_TOKEN automatically — it needs a credential helper configured.

How it manifested: The agent's git push failed: 

fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': No such device
or address

The agent worked around this by manually constructing the URL: 

git push "https://x-access-token:$GH_TOKEN@github.com/owner/repo.git" branch

This works but is fragile and relies on the agent being clever enough to figure it out.

Impact: The agent wasted cycles diagnosing the push failure and constructing the workaround URL. A less capable model might fail the task entirely.

Fix direction: Configure gh as the git credential helper in the Docker image: 

RUN gh auth setup-git

Or configure git to use GH_TOKEN directly: 

RUN git config --global credential.helper \
  '!f() { echo "username=x-access-token"; echo "password=$GH_TOKEN"; }; f'

The gh auth setup-git approach is cleaner — it configures the credential helper to use whatever auth gh has (which reads GH_TOKEN from the environment automatically).

Relevant code: - docker/base/Dockerfile — git/gh configuration - Possibly src/agentrelay/sandbox/implementations/oci_sandbox.py if the setup needs to happen at runtime rather than build time

docs.title: Sprint Notes — 2026-03-10: Architecture Improvements
docs.text: Paused the phase 2 adapter work (sprint 2026-03-09) to address findings from an architecture review of the non-prototype codebase. The review was conducted by Opus 4.6 and documented in docs/reviews/2026-03-10-Opus-46.md.

docs.title: Strengths identified (no action needed)
docs.text:
1. Immutable spec / mutable runtime split — Task, TaskGraph, WorkstreamSpec are frozen; TaskRuntime, WorkstreamRuntime are mutable envelopes. Clean data flow.

  2. TaskRunner state machine — linear lifecycle with explicit transition table. Repetitive try/except around each IO call is correct.

  3. TaskGraph eager validation — no invalid graph can exist. Cycle detection, hierarchy checks, dependency existence all enforced at construction.

  4. Clean protocol boundaries — TaskRunnerIO and adapter protocols map to real operational boundaries.

docs.title: Concerns addressed
docs.text: # Concern Resolution PR 1 Two parallel protocol layers (TaskRunnerIO vs integration_contracts) Decomposed TaskRunnerIO into per-step protocols (TaskPreparer, TaskLauncher, etc.), added WorkstreamRunner/WorkstreamRunnerIO, deleted integration_contracts/ #78 2 Task.dependencies stores Task objects, not IDs Changed to tuple[str, ...], removed redundant validation, simplified builder #79 3 Agent on TaskRuntime is an odd fit Removed live Agent field, added agent_address: AgentAddress to TaskArtifacts as audit trail #80 4 Orchestrator does a lot of direct state mutation Added read-only view protocols (part A), then added mutation methods to runtimes and replaced ~15 direct field assignments in orchestrator (part B) #81, #82 5 integration_errors elaborate but not consumed Renamed to errors/, wired classify_integration_error() into TaskRunner._record_io_failure(), added failure_class to TaskRunResult, orchestrator now distinguishes internal vs expected failures #83 6 RemoteWorkspaceRef is speculative Removed RemoteWorkspaceRef and kind discriminator from LocalWorkspaceRef #84 7 Workstream hierarchy depth — is it needed? Kept as-is. The hierarchy validation and orchestrator ancestor-chain logic will be used when we create nested workstreams. No code change. — 8 _transition_to_failed escape hatch Removed silent fallback that bypassed transition table; method now delegates to _transition() unconditionally after idempotency check #85 9 No logging or observability hooks Added OrchestratorListener protocol with on_event() callback; orchestrator accepts optional listener, notifies at all 6 event emission sites #86

docs.title: Supporting PRs (pre-review, same sprint)
docs.text: PR Description #76 PlantUML diagram infrastructure and conventions cleanup #77 Fix docs deploy: regenerate SVG instead of staleness check

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• 9 review concerns addressed across PRs #78–#86
  • Test count grew from ~600 to 624
  • All changes backward-compatible (no public API removals that affect callers)
  • Codebase is now ready to resume adapter/runner implementation work

docs.title: Sprint Notes — 2026-03-14
docs.text: Status: Complete.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Complete the end-to-end wiring from graph YAML to running orchestrator. All protocol implementations exist; the remaining work is composition, CLI, and cleanup.

docs.title: Context
docs.text: The 2026-03-13 sprint built out all protocol implementations and the agent communication protocol. An unplanned architectural improvement (PR #99) then replaced TaskRunnerIO with per-step StepDispatch[T] dispatch tables on a new StandardTaskRunner, promoted TaskRunner to a protocol, and moved workstream context (integration_branch, workstream_worktree_path) from implementation constructors into TaskState.

docs.title: PR N1: Wire WorkstreamRunner into Orchestrator — Done (PR #101)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/orchestrator-workstream-runner

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Orchestrator accepts workstream_runner parameter. - [x] Workstream prepare/merge/teardown are called at correct lifecycle points. - [x] MERGE_READY intermediate status decouples state detection from merge I/O. - [x] active_task_id removed; active-task constraint derived from task statuses. - [x] fail_fast_on_workstream_error config blocks new workstream preparation. - [x] WorkstreamRunner is a Protocol; StandardWorkstreamRunner is the impl. - [x] All existing orchestrator tests pass (with NoOpWorkstreamRunner double). - [x] Six new lifecycle tests added. - [x] pixi run check passes.

docs.title: PR N2+O: Composition, CLI, and Reset tool — Done (PR #108)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/run-graph-cli
  • New modules: src/agentrelay/run_graph.py, src/agentrelay/reset_graph.py

Originally planned as two separate PRs (N2: composition/CLI, O: reset tool). Consolidated into one PR because run and reset are a matched pair — shipping a CLI to run graphs without a way to reset the repo would be risky.

Scope:

  1. run_graph.py — Top-level composition + CLI:
  2. run_graph() async function wires TaskGraphBuilder, build_standard_runner(), new build_standard_workstream_runner(), and Orchestrator.
  3. CLI via python -m agentrelay.run_graph <graph.yaml> with flags: --max-concurrency, --max-task-attempts, --teardown-mode, --tmux-session, --model, --dry-run.
  4. Writes run_info.json (start HEAD + timestamp) before orchestrator runs.

  5. Operational YAML keys (tmux_session, keep_panes, model) popped from raw dict before graph parsing; CLI flags override YAML values.

  6. reset_graph.py — Graph reset tool:

  7. plan_reset() / execute_reset() separated for testability.
  8. CLI via python -m agentrelay.reset_graph <graph.yaml> with --yes flag.
  9. Closes open PRs, resets main with ancestry safety check, deletes branches, removes worktree and workflow directories.
  10. Out-of-order reset detection skips main-branch reset if unsafe.

  11. Idempotent: re-running after a successful reset is safe.

  12. build_standard_workstream_runner() — New builder in orchestrator/builders.py.

  13. New ops primitives — git.rev_parse_head, git.merge_base_is_ancestor, git.reset_hard, git.push_force_with_lease, git.ls_remote_branches, gh.pr_list, gh.pr_close.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] --dry-run validates graph and prints execution plan. - [x] Integration test verifies full wiring from graph YAML to orchestrator. - [x] Reset is idempotent when re-run. - [x] Reset tested against temp git repo fixture (10 tests). - [x] pixi run check passes (833 tests, 0 errors).

docs.title: Sprint status vs. 2026-03-13 plan
docs.text:
The 2026-03-13 sprint defined PRs K through P. Here's the mapping:

2026-03-13 item Status Notes PR K: Protocol schemas, builders, templates Done (PR #92) PR L: Task-level protocol implementations Done (PR #96) Fix-up: Task preparer branch-only Done (PR #97) PR M: Workstream-level protocol implementations Done (PR #98) (unplanned) StandardTaskRunner dispatch Done (PR #99) Architectural improvement PR N1: Wire WorkstreamRunner into Orchestrator Done (PR #101) (unplanned) Extract _OrchestratorRun Done (PR #102) PR N2: Composition and CLI Done (PR #108) Consolidated with PR O PR O: Reset tool Done (PR #108) Consolidated with PR N2 PR P: Docs and E2E validation Done (PR #109) E2E scripts + demo graphs

docs.title: Merge discipline
docs.text:
1. Complete and merge each PR before starting the next.
  2. pixi run check must pass before every PR.
  3. Each PR includes a ## Test plan checklist in the PR body.
  4. Update docs/HISTORY.md with each merged PR.

docs.title: Sprint Notes — 2026-03-19
docs.text: Status: Complete.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Systematically build out end-to-end test coverage by category. Each category corresponds to a subdirectory under graphs/ with YAML graphs, README docs, and observations from test runs. Fix bugs and wire unimplemented features as needed to enable each test category.

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
E2E testing infrastructure is in place (e2e_run.sh, e2e_reset.sh, e2e_check.sh). Smoke tests exist in graphs/smoke/. Real-time console output and verbose mode were added in PR #112. The dispatch race condition was fixed in PR #111 and the outcome reporting bug in PR #113.

Investigation revealed several features that are scaffolded (parsed from YAML, written to policies.json) but not wired in the runner: completion gates, review, and ADR production. A concerns bug was also found (signal.concerns dropped in the runner).

docs.title: PR A: Agent-side helper + fix concerns bug + concerns/ test graphs — Done (PR #114)
docs.text:
• Branch: fix/concerns-capture

Agent-side helper: Create an agent-side helper class that agents instantiate inside their tmux session to interact with the orchestrator via a clean Python API. Replaces raw shell commands (echo ... > .done) with method calls (helper.mark_done(pr_url)). Reads task metadata from manifest.json in $AGENTRELAY_SIGNAL_DIR.

Methods: - mark_done(pr_url) — write .done signal - mark_failed(reason) — write .failed signal - record_concern(concern) — append to concerns.log - Class method from_env() — instantiate from $AGENTRELAY_SIGNAL_DIR

Update the workflow footer in agent_comm_protocol/templates.py to instruct agents to use the helper API instead of raw shell commands. Include concern recording guidance for all roles (optional — "if you have concerns, record them").

Bug fix (concerns transfer): - StandardTaskRunner.run() reads signal.concerns from the completion checker but never transfers them to runtime.artifacts.concerns. Add the missing assignment. (Already implemented on fix/concerns-capture branch.)

Test graphs: - graphs/concerns/README.md — category docs - Graph with a task whose description instructs the agent to record concerns via the helper API. Verify they appear in the orchestrator result.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Agent-side helper class works when imported by agent in tmux session - [x] Workflow footer uses helper API for completion signaling and concerns - [x] Concerns from concerns.log are stored in runtime.artifacts.concerns - [x] Concerns appear in console output - [x] pixi run check passes - [x] E2E: concerns graph runs, agent uses helper, concerns are captured

docs.title: PR A2: Rich integration PR + stop auto-merging to main — Done (PR #115)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/integration-pr-summary

Stop auto-merging to main: - GhWorkstreamMerger currently creates the integration PR and immediately merges it via gh pr merge. Change to create-only — the PR is left open for human review and merge. (The prototype also left this to humans.) - This avoids silent failures from merge conflicts when parallel workstreams modify overlapping files.

Enrich integration PR body: - Build a rich PR body that includes: - List of tasks in the workstream with descriptions and PR URLs - Aggregated design concerns from all tasks - Pass task summaries to the merger so it can build the body (currently it only receives the WorkstreamRuntime with no task-level info).

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Integration PR is created but NOT auto-merged - [x] Integration PR body lists all tasks with PR links - [x] Design concerns from all tasks appear in the integration PR body - [x] pixi run check passes - [x] E2E: integration PR on GitHub has a meaningful summary and is open (not merged)

docs.title: PR A3: Signal-file-backed TaskStatus (match workstream pattern) — Done (PR #117)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/task-signal-files

Align task status management with the workstream signal file pattern: - Establish a task-level signal directory convention under .workflow/<graph>/signals/<task_id>/status/ with files matching TaskStatus enum values (pending, running, pr_created, pr_merged, failed). - Make TaskStatus a computed property on TaskRuntime, derived from which signal files exist (same pattern as WorkstreamRuntime after PR A2). - This should be done soon after PR A2 so that task and workstream status management strategies don't diverge for long.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Task status determined from signal files, same pattern as workstreams - [x] Existing task lifecycle behavior unchanged - [x] pixi run check passes

docs.title: PR B1: roles/ pipeline graph + implementer template fix — Done (PR #118)
docs.text:
• Branch: test/role-graphs

Delivered: - graphs/roles/pipeline.yaml — four-role pipeline (spec_writer → test_writer → test_reviewer → implementer) building a BoundedQueue class. Includes a deliberate spec inconsistency (eviction vs OverflowError) and a completion gate on the implementer. - Updated templates/implementer.md to connect concern documentation to helper.record_concern() with guidance on what qualifies as a concern. - Backlog items: structured instruction architecture, agent build environment awareness (including ops concerns concept and TaskHelper CLI wrapper).

E2E result: - Pipeline runs to completion. All four roles produce expected artifacts. - Implementer does NOT organically record a concern about the spec inconsistency (with Sonnet, baseline instructions). Concern instruction refinement deferred to PR BN.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Each role produces expected artifacts (specs, tests, implementations) - [x] Multi-role pipeline: changes propagate through the chain - [ ] ~~Implementer organically records a concern~~ (deferred to PR BN) - [x] pixi run check passes - [x] E2E: pipeline graph runs to completion

docs.title: PR B2: Declared tools + TaskHelper CLI + template cleanup — Done (PRs #120, #121)
docs.text:
• Branches: feat/declared-tools-cli, fix/template-description-cleanup

Delivered: - Declared tools field in graph YAML (list of tool names). Orchestrator validates each tool is available (preflight check) and injects usage guidance into agent instructions via TOOL_REGISTRY in tools.py. - TaskHelper CLI wrapper: agentrelay-complete, agentrelay-failed, agentrelay-concern — shell commands replacing inline Python. - Workflow footer updated to show CLI commands + tools guidance. - Optional $description_section in all role templates — task description now renders directly in instructions.md when present. - Implementer concern guidance aligned to CLI (agentrelay-concern). - Backlog items: instruction architecture trade-offs, role-specific workflow issues (no-commit PR, git push upstream, spec source-of-truth).

E2E result: - Pipeline runs to completion. Agents use pixi run and complete via CLI.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Agents consistently use the correct build system commands - [x] TaskHelper completion works via CLI without shell-escaping issues - [x] Task description appears in spec_writer instructions.md - [x] pixi run check passes (897 tests) - [x] E2E: agents use pixi run and complete tasks without env friction

docs.title: PR B3: Role-specific workflow fixes — Done (PR #125)
docs.text:
• Branch: fix/role-workflow

Delivered: - Agent-driven PR-less completion: NO_PR_SENTINEL, complete_without_pr(), agentrelay-complete-no-pr CLI. Runner skips merge when pr_url is None (RUNNING → PR_MERGED). Workflow footer and test_reviewer template updated. - push.autoSetupRemote=true set in worktree git config during workstream preparation via new git.set_config() ops wrapper. - Also landed PR #126: save task PR body as summary.md in signal directory (fetched after PR_CREATED, before merge; preserved if merge fails).

E2E result: - Pipeline completes in ~4 min. test_reviewer uses agentrelay-complete-no-pr cleanly. No agent needs git push -u. spec_writer catches deliberate spec inconsistency and records concern organically.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Review-only tasks complete without needing a dummy commit - [x] First git push succeeds without --set-upstream - [x] pixi run check passes (907 tests) - [x] E2E: pipeline runs without either workaround

docs.title: PR B4: Ops concerns — Done (PR #127)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/ops-concerns

Delivered: - Separate ops concerns channel: record_ops_concern() on TaskHelper + agentrelay-ops-concern CLI command. Stored in ops_concerns.log in the signal directory (parallel to concerns.log for design concerns). - End-to-end pipeline: TaskHelper.record_ops_concern() → ops_concerns.log → SignalCompletionChecker → TaskArtifacts.ops_concerns → TaskSummary.ops_concerns → integration PR body (## Ops Concerns) + console summary (Ops Concerns: block). - Individual task PRs also get a ## Ops Concerns section when ops concerns are present. - Workflow footer updated: step 2 now distinguishes design vs ops concerns with category labels and examples.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Agents can record ops concerns via TaskHelper - [x] Ops concerns appear separately from design concerns in console output - [x] Ops concerns appear in integration PR body - [x] pixi run check passes (919 tests) - [x] E2E: agent records an ops concern, visible in output

docs.title: PR BN: Concern instruction refinement — Done (PR #129)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/concern-refinement

Delivered: - Cross-check steps added to all four role templates so agents actively look for spec contradictions. General concerns note placed before numbered steps via $concerns_note placeholder (scope → how to record → steps). - Wording refined: "throughout" emphasis in general note, "if not already recorded" in specific steps, clearer CLI command guidance. - Single-task experiment graphs (graphs/roles/experiments/) with BoundedQueue fixtures for isolated per-role testing. Setup script with stubs/all modes. - Console concern display improved: task ID on its own line, concern text wrapped at 76 chars with indentation.

E2E result: - "Prompted" guidance level works reliably with Sonnet across all four roles. All agents caught the deliberate BoundedQueue contradiction (push evicts vs raises OverflowError) and recorded design concerns.

Deferred to backlog: - Full model × guidance level matrix (Haiku, Opus) and guidance level experimentation (minimal, checklist). See BACKLOG.md.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] At least one guidance level reliably produces organic concern discovery - [x] Guidance is documented and integrated into templates - [x] pixi run check passes (935 tests) - [ ] ~~Results matrix (model × guidance level)~~ (deferred to backlog)

docs.title: PR C: failure/ and workstreams/ test graphs — Done (PR #131)
docs.text:
• Branch: test/failure-and-workstream-graphs

Delivered: - graphs/failure/ category: agent_fails.yaml (single task deliberately signals .failed) and blocked_downstream.yaml (failure propagation + dependency blocking + independent workstream continues). Retry graph deferred to PR D where completion gates provide natural retry scenarios. - graphs/workstreams/ category: diamond_1_workstream.yaml (fan-in in shared worktree), diamond_4_workstreams.yaml (cross-workstream fan-in, 4 separate workstreams), serial_workstream.yaml (3 zero-dependency tasks serialized by workstream constraint). - Backlog items from e2e observations: human retry intervention, fail-fast CLI flags, agent isolation, Bifrost/OpenRouter multi-model support, Rust migration. Discussion reference docs added (docs/discussions/).

E2E observations: - blocked_downstream requires --max-concurrency 2 so both workstreams are prepared before the failure. Default fail_fast_on_workstream_error=True blocks new workstream preparation after a failure (backlogged: CLI flag). - diamond_4_workstreams: agents discovered missing upstream code and autonomously merged the dependency's branch into their own worktree. Did not raise ops concerns despite the workaround. Confirms the cross-workstream code visibility gap (backlogged).

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Failed task produces correct outcome and error messages - [x] Blocked tasks emit task_blocked event - [ ] ~~Retry with max_task_attempts: 2~~ (deferred to PR D) - [x] Diamond topology executes correctly (both single and 4-workstream) - [x] pixi run check passes (935 tests) - [x] E2E: at least one graph from each category runs

docs.title: PR D: Wire completion gates + gates/ test graphs — Done (PR #132)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/completion-gates

Wire gate execution: - TaskGateChecker protocol + GateCheckResult in task_runner/core/io.py - ShellGateChecker implementation: runs shell command in worktree, captures output to gate_last_output.txt, returns pass/fail - Gate step in StandardTaskRunner.run(): after completion signal + PR_CREATED, before merge. Retry loop up to max_gate_attempts (default 5) - Gate failure marks task FAILED with failure_class=None (retryable by orchestrator when max_task_attempts allows) - Events: task_gate_running (verbose), task_gate_passed (verbose), task_gate_failed (always visible) - Direct field on runner (not StepDispatch) — gate doesn't vary by framework/environment

Test graphs: - graphs/gates/ category with pixi_run_test.yaml (gate-pass scenario) - graphs/failure/retry_on_gate_failure.yaml (gate-triggered retry with --max-task-attempts 2)

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Gate command runs after task signals .done - [x] Gate failure triggers retry (up to max_gate_attempts) - [x] Gate pass proceeds to PR merge - [ ] ~~Retry: agent's second attempt passes the gate after first attempt fails~~ (deferred — requires multi-attempt e2e) - [x] Verbose output shows gate events - [x] pixi run check passes (957 tests) - [x] E2E: gate graph runs, tests pass, PR merges

docs.title: PR E: Wire ADR production + adr/ test graphs — Done (PR #133)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/adr-production

Delivered: - Rename AgentVerbosity → AdrVerbosity for naming clarity (consistent with _AdrPolicy). - _adr_section(verbosity, task_id) in templates.py: builds a verbosity-scaled ## Architecture Decision Record section with output path docs/adr/<task_id>.md. Three tiers: STANDARD (5-section Nygard format), DETAILED (+Alternatives, Trade-offs, Implementation Notes), EDUCATIONAL (+annotations explaining each section). - resolve_instructions() gains adr_verbosity parameter; section injected between "What to Do" and "Submitting Your Work" when verbosity != NONE. - WorktreeTaskPreparer threads task.primary_agent.adr_verbosity to resolve_instructions(). - graphs/adr/ category with adr_standard.yaml test graph.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Agent produces ADR when verbosity != none - [x] ADR detail scales with verbosity level - [x] pixi run check passes (975 tests) - [ ] E2E: adr graph runs, ADR file is present in committed output

PR F (agent-assisted integration branch merging) was originally planned for this sprint but deferred to a future sprint. See docs/BACKLOG.md under "Agent-Assisted Integration Branch Merging" for the full design sketch.

docs.title: Sprint Notes — 2026-03-25
docs.text: Status: In Progress.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Wire cross-workstream dependency ordering so that tasks depending on work in other workstreams are not dispatched prematurely. Enable the orchestrator to detect when integration PRs are merged (initially via GitHub polling), and optionally auto-merge integration PRs when conditions are met.

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
The orchestrator dispatches a task when all its DAG dependencies have task-level status PR_MERGED (merged into the dependency's workstream integration branch). This is correct for same-workstream dependencies, where tasks share an integration branch. But for cross-workstream dependencies, the upstream workstream's integration PR must also be merged to main — otherwise the downstream task's worktree (branched from main) is missing the dependency's code.

During e2e testing (sprint 2026-03-19, diamond_4_workstreams.yaml), an agent in Workstream Y discovered its upstream code was missing, found the Workstream X integration PR awaiting human review, and merged it unilaterally. The task succeeded, but the coordination model was violated: a human-reviewed PR was merged without approval, and the agent didn't report the workaround. See docs/discussions/AGENT_ISOLATION_REFINED.md for full analysis.

docs.title: Architecture notes
docs.text:
Current dispatch pipeline (three checkpoints in orchestrator.py):

Function What it checks TaskGraph.ready_ids() Task-level DAG dependencies (pure graph, no workstream awareness) _workstream_can_run() Workstream state, serialization, parent hierarchy _blocked_reason() Permanent blockers: failed deps, failed/pending workstreams

The cross-workstream check fits into _workstream_can_run() or nearby.

Integration PR lifecycle:

  1. All tasks in workstream complete → MERGE_READY
  2. Orchestrator creates integration PR → PR_CREATED
  3. Human merges PR to main → MERGED (currently no detection mechanism)

Step 3 is the gap — the orchestrator has no way to know when the human has merged the PR. This sprint adds polling-based detection (PR A) and optional orchestrator-driven merge (PR B).

Platform coupling: GitHub-specific code is isolated behind protocols (TaskMerger, WorkstreamIntegrator, etc.) with concrete Gh* implementations. The new IntegrationMergeChecker protocol follows the same pattern, keeping the orchestrator platform-agnostic.

docs.title: PR A: Cross-workstream dispatch gating + integration PR merge detection
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/cross-workstream-ordering
  • Merged: PR #134

What shipped: - gh.pr_is_merged(pr_url) -> bool in ops/gh.py — returns False on CalledProcessError (defensive for polling). - IntegrationMergeChecker protocol in workstream/core/io.py + GhIntegrationMergeChecker in workstream/implementations/. - TaskGraph.upstream_workstream_ids(task_id) — pure graph query for cross-workstream dependency resolution. - Cross-workstream gate in _workstream_can_run() — upstream workstreams must be MERGED before dispatch. - _blocked_reason() distinguishes "waiting for merge" (transient) from "no checker configured" (permanent deadlock). - Polling loop: _poll_integration_merges() checks PR_CREATED workstreams each iteration; _has_pending_integration_merges() prevents false deadlock; asyncio.wait() timeout enables polling while tasks are in-flight. - merge_poll_interval on OrchestratorConfig (default 30s). - GitWorkstreamPreparer fetches + updates local base branch ref before creating worktrees (discovered during e2e: local main was stale after remote merge). - Console events: workstream_merged, waiting_for_integration_merge. - 23 new tests (975 → 998).

Acceptance criteria: - [x] Cross-workstream task dispatch blocked until upstream workstream MERGED - [x] Orchestrator detects human-merged integration PR via gh pr view - [x] Same-workstream dependencies unaffected (no behavior change) - [x] pixi run check passes - [x] E2E: cross-workstream graph blocks correctly, proceeds after human merge

E2E observations: - First run (before base branch fetch fix): square_calc reported ops concern that base_calc.py was missing — local main was stale after remote merge. Fixed by adding fetch_branch + update_local_ref in GitWorkstreamPreparer. - Second run (with fix): all 4 tasks succeeded cleanly, no ops concerns, no agent workarounds. Total time 3m13s.

docs.title: PR B: Orchestrator-driven integration PR merge (optional auto-merge)
docs.text:
• Branch: feat/auto-merge-integration
  • Merged: PR #135

WorkstreamSpec extension: - auto_merge: bool field on WorkstreamSpec (default: false). - YAML parsing + validation in task_graph/builder.py.

Auto-merge logic: - When a workstream reaches MERGE_READY and auto_merge is true: 1. Check all tasks in the workstream for design concerns. If any exist, skip auto-merge (fall back to human review). 2. Create the integration PR (existing behavior). 3. Merge the integration PR via gh.pr_merge(). 4. Transition workstream to MERGED. - When auto_merge is false (default), behavior unchanged — integration PR created and left for human review.

Concern-gated merge: - Auto-merge is conditional on zero design concerns across the workstream's tasks. If any task recorded a design concern, the integration PR is created but NOT auto-merged, regardless of the auto_merge setting. This ensures human review when agents flag potential issues.

Tests: - Unit tests for auto-merge logic (with and without concerns) - Unit tests for YAML parsing of auto_merge field - Tests verifying concern-gated behavior (concerns present → no auto-merge)

E2E validation: - New graph in graphs/workstreams/ with auto_merge: true — verify the integration PR is merged automatically when no concerns exist. - Variant where an agent records a concern — verify auto-merge is skipped.

What shipped: - auto_merge: bool field on WorkstreamSpec (default: false). - YAML parsing + boolean validation in task_graph/builder.py. - IntegrationAutoMerger protocol in workstream/core/io.py + GhIntegrationAutoMerger in workstream/implementations/. - Concern-gated auto-merge in _process_merge_ready_workstreams() via new _try_auto_merge() method — checks task_summaries for design concerns before calling merge. - Console events: workstream_auto_merged, workstream_auto_merge_skipped, workstream_auto_merge_failed. - Wired into composition: build_integration_auto_merger() builder, integration_auto_merger field on Orchestrator. - 18 new tests (998 → 1016). - E2E graphs: auto_merge_2_workstreams.yaml, diamond_4_workstreams_auto_merge.yaml.

Acceptance criteria: - [x] auto_merge: true causes orchestrator to merge integration PR - [x] Auto-merge skipped when design concerns exist - [x] auto_merge: false (default) preserves current human-review behavior - [x] pixi run check passes - [x] E2E: auto-merge graph completes without human intervention

E2E observations: - auto_merge_2_workstreams.yaml: ws_a auto-merged successfully, ws_b completed without human intervention. Confirmed concern-gate not triggered when no concerns exist. - diamond_4_workstreams_auto_merge.yaml: full diamond (4 workstreams, cross-workstream dependencies) completed hands-free with all integration PRs auto-merged.
